title: Imhofia rosea
intro: Imhofia rosea, also known as Sabine's passion flower, is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Passifloraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and as an anti-inflammatory agent, due to its ability to reduce pain and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Imhofia rosea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Imhofia rosea is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal use, and it is also used in traditional herbal remedies such as teas and tinctures.
